message:
Extract document field extraction into helper functions

Refactored the search result processing loop by extracting repetitive
document field extraction logic into dedicated helper functions, improving
readability and maintainability.

Changes:

1. Added Value to tantivy::schema imports
   - Required for explicit type annotation in doc_text() helper
   - Clarifies that v is tantivy::schema::Value, not arbitrary value

2. Renamed final_query to q
   - Shorter variable name for temporary query box
   - Only used once (immediately passed to searcher.search())
   - Common Rust pattern for short-lived variables
   - Improves line length (previous name was somewhat verbose)

3. Extracted doc_title() helper
   - Signature: fn doc_title(doc: &Document, field: Field) -> String
   - Returns title field text content or empty string
   - Encapsulates the get_first().and_then().unwrap_or() pattern

4. Extracted doc_snippet() helper
   - Signature: fn doc_snippet(doc: &Document, field: Field) -> String
   - Returns first line of content field or empty string
   - Delegates to doc_text() then applies .lines().next()
   - Centralizes snippet extraction logic for future enhancement
     (e.g., could add character limit, highlight matching terms)

5. Extracted doc_text() helper
   - Signature: fn doc_text(doc: &Document, field: Field) -> String
   - Generic text field extractor used by both title and snippet
   - Added explicit type annotation: |v: &Value|
   - Returns field text content or empty string

Result processing simplification:

Before (inline field extraction):
```rust
let title = doc
    .get_first(self.fields.title)
    .and_then(|v| v.as_text())
    .unwrap_or("")
    .to_string();
let content = doc
    .get_first(self.fields.content)
    .and_then(|v| v.as_text())
    .unwrap_or("")
    .to_string();
let snippet = content.lines().next().unwrap_or("").to_string();
let source = doc
    .get_first(self.fields.source_path)
    .and_then(|v| v.as_text())
    .unwrap_or("")
    .to_string();
```

After (helper function calls):
```rust
let title = doc_title(&doc, self.fields.title);
let snippet = doc_snippet(&doc, self.fields.content);
let source = doc_text(&doc, self.fields.source_path);
```

Benefits of this refactoring:

1. Readability
   - The search loop now clearly shows what's being extracted
   - Helper names (doc_title, doc_snippet) are self-documenting
   - Reduced visual noise from repetitive and_then/unwrap_or chains

2. Reusability
   - doc_text() can be used for any text field extraction
   - doc_snippet() can be enhanced (character limit, highlighting) in one place
   - Future features (e.g., different snippet strategies) only change helpers

3. Testability
   - Helper functions can be unit tested independently
   - Could be made pub(crate) for testing from other modules
   - Easier to verify edge cases (missing fields, empty content, etc.)

4. Maintainability
   - Changing field extraction behavior (e.g., trimming whitespace) only
     requires modifying the relevant helper function
   - Adding new extracted fields (e.g., workspace, agent) follows same pattern

The helper functions follow Rust conventions:
- Private module-level functions (not methods on SearchClient)
- Take explicit parameters rather than capturing self
- Return owned String for simple API (caller doesn't need lifetimes)
- Use Field directly (opaque type from Tantivy, Copy-able)

This refactoring prepares the code for future enhancements like:
- Custom snippet generation (character limits, ellipsis)
- Highlighted snippets (marking query term matches)
- Fallback content when fields are missing
- Different snippet strategies (multiple lines, context windows)
